# This allows dotnet-symbol to look up symbols by build ID

buildid_hex = "4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"
buildid_bytes = bytes.fromhex(buildid_hex)

# ELF Header
EI_MAG = b'\x7fELF'
EI_CLASS = 2  # ELFCLASS64
EI_DATA = 1   # ELFDATA2LSB
EI_VERSION = 1
EI_OSABI = 0
e_type = 3    # ET_DYN (shared object)
e_machine = 183  # EM_AARCH64
e_version = 1
e_entry = 0
e_flags = 0
e_ehsize = 64
e_phentsize = 56
e_phnum = 1   # One program header (PT_NOTE)
e_shentsize = 64
e_shnum = 0
e_shstrndx = 0

# Build the note section content
# Note: namesz, descsz, type, name, desc
note_name = b'GNU\x00'
note_type = 3  # NT_GNU_BUILD_ID
namesz = len(note_name)
descsz = len(buildid_bytes)

note_data = struct.pack('<III', namesz, descsz, note_type)
note_data += note_name
# Pad name to 4-byte alignment
while len(note_data) % 4:
    note_data += b'\x00'
note_data += buildid_bytes
# Pad desc to 4-byte alignment
while len(note_data) % 4:
    note_data += b'\x00'

# Calculate offsets
phoff = e_ehsize  # Program headers start right after ELF header
note_offset = phoff + e_phentsize * e_phnum

# Program Header (PT_NOTE)
PT_NOTE = 4
p_type = PT_NOTE
p_flags = 4   # PF_R
p_offset = note_offset
p_vaddr = 0
p_paddr = 0
p_filesz = len(note_data)
p_memsz = len(note_data)
p_align = 4

# Assemble ELF header
elf = b''
elf += EI_MAG
elf += struct.pack('BBBBB', EI_CLASS, EI_DATA, EI_VERSION, EI_OSABI, 0)
elf += b'\x00' * 7  # EI_PAD
elf += struct.pack('<HHIQ', e_type, e_machine, e_version, e_entry)
elf += struct.pack('<QQ', phoff, 0)  # e_phoff, e_shoff
elf += struct.pack('<IHHHHHH', e_flags, e_ehsize, e_phentsize, e_phnum, e_shentsize, e_shnum, e_shstrndx)

# Assemble program header
elf += struct.pack('<IIQQQQQQ', p_type, p_flags, p_offset, p_vaddr, p_paddr, p_filesz, p_memsz, p_align)

# Append note data
elf += note_data

with open('/tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(elf)

print(f"Created ELF stub with Build ID: {buildid_hex}")
print(f"File size: {len(elf)} bytes")
